All right, your back. Let's roll forward here and get into the subtleties of wrapping your package. Stawning with things like facial hair. Now, as a general rule for a man, the less facial hair, the better. Anything more than a very closely cropped beer or trim mustache and you'll be considered untrustworthy. Bottom line. Now, if you're a woman with a beard, well, then go see your doctor, right? But the point is here, as a man, you want to be very careful not to blow your ability to get into a rapport. Be trusted by having a long, scraggly beard of any sort of thing. There's really too much facial hair. People say, you know, I don't know, this person, like, they don't seem to take pride in their appearance. They're not, you know, you know, they're not like, kent the right way, so to speak, right? And because of that, it just makes it very difficult for you to build rapport with someone. Now, that being said, like anything else, there are exceptions to the rule. For instance, if you're selling Holly Davidson's, right? You know, you could walk into a Holly Davidson's and see a guy with a nice big beard and little beads on it and you're like, damn, that's the guy. So which bite should I recommend? Then it makes sense. So again, you always want to be smart about this. So for instance, for a woman now, it's shift to women, what would be the equivalent of a big unruly beard? One of those befond beehive hairdos. In other words, you look at it and you're like, what the heck? Like, it's something about that's off. In other words, we just want to appear to be essentially within the normal range of what's expected from someone who's an expert in the field. So women again, you know, whatever, there's no right or wrong hairdo, but if it's an extreme hairdo, it takes away from who you are. It doesn't, it doesn't add, it takes away. Same thing goes with jewelry. Now a woman has nice earrings on, that's fine. But if she has gigantic hoop earrings on, what do you think? You're like, huh, what's wrong with this personally? And they should not, again, if you're going out on a Saturday night, that's a very different story. But in the workplace as an influencer, you know, those gigantic hoop earrings aren't going to really go over that unless of course you work in a store that sells jewelry and you're wearing things to stand out. So there's again, always exceptions to the rule, but in general, you know, again, the less jewelry, the better. Let me tell you as a man, what's the worst piece of jewelry you can possibly have and you can possibly wear? Tell you what it is, ready? A pinky ring, a big diamond pinky ring. As soon as a woman lays eyes on that ring or even a man, they're like, you know what? Huh, I don't trust that person. I never, I never sharpie, did something wrong with them. You will not be trusted. It's something about a pinky ring that just kind of breeds contempt. Now again, the exception, what if you were a pick-boss in a casino? Then you see a guy, oh wow, look at this cool pink ring. I want to buy one if I win money downstairs in the jewelry store. You get it, so there's always exceptions. So be smart about this. But in general, a pinky ring is very toxic. The same way, if you're wearing a solid gold thick Rolex watch with diamonds around it, that does not make the right impression in the business world. Because of course you own a jewelry store and you sell Rolex watches and you want to have something crazy there or in a casino setting. But for the most part, that ostentatious that over the top jewelry simply takes away. Now let's talk about geography, about beers. It doesn't work well in the Western world, but if you're in the Middle East, in fact, not having a beard can be considered to be, you know, out of, put you out of sorts, not be trusted. Again, it's always about where do you live, what's considered normal in your geographic area and then try to fit in that way. That's the best way I can explain it. Also, along the lines, perfume, cologne, how much do you wear? If you're a woman and you're just drenched in perfume, you know, what men, I'll tell you as a man, to me, I'm like, you know, I'd like to be away from that woman because it just kind of grosses me out. It's just sending a signal that, you know, not being judged for you, not being based on your intellect and what you know and your integrity, it's just, it's over. It's too much in your face. A little bit of perfume, yeah, great, fine. Of course, everyone wants to, you know, men can be men, women and women. There's some always sexual tension in the world, right? But you know, be, be liberal with this stuff. And men especially, dealt with the cologne, don't drench yourself in cologne. Women hate that. I can tell you, I've asked, I've asked this of many women. They've always said, you know, a man with too much cologne, take a hike. I don't want that person even near me. It says that you're on the make, that you're looking to, you're looking to get away with something, you know, just pull a wool over someone's eyes. Now let's take this to a different level here. I want to be cautious here, remember your ethics and integrity, but there are certain things that we can do as a man that really help us to be trusted by others. For instance, you have a wallet and you pull out your wallet and there's a picture of your beautiful family, your wife and your two kids there and spot the dog. And also what's the best piece of jewelry you're wearing? You have a wedding band on and you have a nice conservative watch with a leather strap on it, right? Well, there are some people who teach body language who say you want to make this part of your, of your whole shtick where you want to actually pull, give yourself a chance, pull your wallet, let them see the picture of the family. Okay, and by the way, even if you don't have a family, invent the family, invent the dog, put a ring on, don't do that. That's my point. So I don't want you to invent the family, don't put a wedding ring on because what does that really say? Well, one person trusts you, then you're obviously trustworthy. You're in a trusting relationship. That's what that says. So a wedding ring can be a very powerful, you know, signal that you're trustworthy. But again, you know, this is about making the best of your actual circumstance, not inventing a circumstance. So don't go out there and buy a wedding ring and don't, you know, put a fake picture of a family in your wallet. But I want you to save yourself. You know, what can I do? What can I show that really gives the appearance of who I really am as my best self? What's real, not imagined, what's not made up? But if you can put those things into the equation, they're very powerful. So again, if you're married or you're in a relationship with someone, you want to pull that picture out and show the family with the dog and your children is awesome by the way. Watch, best watch of all is going to be a stainless steel watch, no gold leather, leather band, women, standard earrings, nothing crazy, hint of perfume, not too much. It all kind of makes sense. I know these are basically the rules of body language of wrapping your package that basically say I'm normal. I'm okay. I'm just like you. I can be trusted. I have nothing to prove. I am who I am. I'm a more authentic, the real person. That's why it's so powerful. So with that, let's take a quick quiz here. I want to just go through a whole bunch of questions with you that are better if I can do this more like in the classroom style than just on camera here. And then when we come back, I want to kick things up a notch and show you how to apply this in actual, in movement, meaning how you actually start using body language to move your body in a certain way to make people feel more comfortable to open them up and also how you can use something called pacing and leading to essentially take someone from one state of mind to another, from a state of uncertainty to a state of certainty, from a state of being angry to a state of calm, very, very powerful. So let's take a break now. Do your quiz when you come back. We'll kick things up a notch and I'll see you then.
